steps.title: Identify the sentence structure
steps.content: The sentence already has a finite main verb: "adapted the test." The blank comes inside a supplementary phrase modifying scientists. A second finite verb in that slot would create an ungrammatical run-on; a nonfinite present participle is needed to open the participial modifier.

steps.title: Test each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'searched': finite past tense verb. Creates two finite verbs ("searched"... "adapted") with no connector — ungrammatical. ✗
  • Choice B — 'searching': nonfinite present participle. Correctly opens the supplementary phrase "searching for evidence of self-awareness in snakes," which modifies scientists while "adapted" remains the lone main verb. ✓
  • Choice C — 'were searching': finite past progressive. Same problem as A — produces two competing finite verbs. ✗
  • Choice D — 'have searched': finite present perfect. Again a second finite verb with no connector — ungrammatical. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
